title: Ecce homo, Wie man wird, was man ist
summaries: "Ecce homo, Wie man wird, was man ist" by Friedrich Nietzsche is a philosophical work written in 1888. In this audacious self-examination, Nietzsche offers his own interpretation of his life, works, and significance through provocatively titled chapters like "Why I Am So Wise" and "Why I Write Such Good Books." Part philosophical defense, part self-portrait, the book presents Nietzsche's vision of what it means to become who you truly are, while reviewing his major works and challenging conventional morality and Christianity with characteristic boldness.
